title: Sysco A Bit Too Pricey
text: Sysco has seen a long road to normalcy from pandemic lows. Now in 2022 the business is starting to resume pre-pandemic operations.

title: Sysco: Upside Is Still On The Table
text: Sysco has shown strong growth since the pandemic came to an end, with sales and profits rising nicely. The current trend looks set to continue for now, which is great for adding value to the company's shareholders.

title: Sysco's (SYY) Latest Move to Enhance Delivery to Aid Growth
text: Sysco (SYY) and Daimler Truck North America unveil a Letter of Intent to allocate up to around 800 battery electric Freightliner eCascadia Class 8 tractors to cater to Sysco's U.S. customers by 2026.

title: 3 Long-Haul Dividend Kings Under $100
text: Now is a great time to stock your portfolio with companies that provide the products and services we need -- not want.

title: Sysco (SYY) Looks Promising: Stock Up More Than 10% in 6 Months
text: Sysco Corporation (SYY) has been gaining on its Recipe for Growth plan and solid food-away-from-home trends.

title: Sysco Corporation (SYY) CEO Kevin Hourican on Q3 2022 Results - Earnings Call Transcript
text: Sysco Corporation (NYSE:SYY ) Q3 2022 Results Conference Call May 10, 2022 10:00 AM ET Company Participants Kevin Kim - Vice President of Investor Relations Kevin Hourican - President & Chief Executive Officer Aaron Alt - Chief Financial Officer Neil Russell - SVP of Corporate Affairs and Chief Communications Officer Conference Call Participants Lauren Silberman - Credit Suisse Alex Slagle - Jefferies Julio Marquez - Guggenheim Jake Bartlett - Truist Securities Edward Kelly - Wells Fargo Jeffrey Bernstein - Barclays Kelly Bania - BMO Capital Markets John Ivankoe - JPMorgan Nicole Miller - Piper Sandler Operator Good morning, and welcome to Sysco's Third Quarter Fiscal 2022 Conference Call. As a reminder, today's call is being recorded.

title: Sysco Shares Pop On Q3 Beat, Raised FY22 Outlook
text: Sysco Corp (NYSE: SYY) reported third-quarter FY22 sales growth of 42.9% year-on-year to $16.90 billion, beating the consensus of $15.91 billion. The U.S. Foodservice operations sales rose 43.6% Y/Y, and International Foodservice sales grew 64.5%.

title: Sysco (SYY) Beats on Q3 Earnings & Sales, Raises EPS View
text: Sysco's (SYY) third-quarter fiscal 2022 results reflect increased top and bottom lines. The company benefits from its Recipe for Growth plan and efforts to manage cost inflation.

title: Sysco (SYY) Q3 Earnings and Revenues Top Estimates
text: Sysco (SYY) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 31.48% and 5.21%, respectively, for the quarter ended March 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Sysco shares jump as demand for food away from home grows
text: Sysco Corp. SYY, -1.70% shares jumped 3.6% in Tuesday premarket trading after it reported fiscal third-quarter profit and sales that beat expectations. The food distributor posted net income of $303.3 million, or 59 cents per share, up from $88.9 million, or 17 cents per share, last year.

title: 5 Picks for May From Best S&P 500 Sectors in Ruthless April
text: We have narrowed our search to five consumer staples stocks that have solid growth potential for the rest of 2022. These are: PPC, ADM, CHEF, MKC and SYY.
